commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=64feeb549fe9969d4b3281caac57879827f3ef11)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes memory-core identity comparison and pre-init provider fallback handling so empty provider models become `fts-only`, and adds three identity-state unit tests.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +33. Total +33 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the narrow empty-string path from source: current main reads `params.provider.model` directly, so `model: ""` produces an empty expected model. I did not reproduce the full upgrade/runtime failure in a live setup during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review should request the provider-boundary repair and contributor-side real behavior proof; automation cannot supply the contributor’s real setup proof for this external PR.

target.title: [AI] fix(memory): prevent empty-string expectedModel in resolveMemory…
target.body:
## Summary

`resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState` computes `expectedModel` from `params.provider.model`, but when `provider.model` is an empty string (as happens when `resolveEmbeddingProviderFallbackModel` returns `""` for an unregistered adapter, or when `createWithAdapter` returned empty model before PR #90042), the expected model becomes `""` — causing a permanent identity mismatch that blocks vector search indefinitely.

Before this fix, `expectedModel` was computed as:
```ts
params.provider ? params.provider.model : "fts-only"
```
This falls through to `"fts-only"` only when `provider` is `null`. When `provider` exists but `provider.model` is `""` (empty string), `expectedModel` becomes `""`, producing a mismatch reason like "index was built for model gemini-embedding-001, expected " — the trailing empty string makes recovery impossible.

After this fix:
```ts
params.provider?.model?.trim() || "fts-only"
```
Empty or whitespace-only `provider.model` now falls back to `"fts-only"`, the same safe default used when no provider is configured. An existing fts-only index matches cleanly; a non-fts index is mismatched with a readable reason ("expected fts-only") instead of a broken one ("expected ").

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] Memory / storage

## Linked Issue

- Related to #90787 (prevents empty-string expectedModel in identity check; provider auto→openai migration decision is a separate product issue)
- Related to #90042 (provider.model backfill in createWithAdapter; this PR protects the identity check even when backfill hasn't happened yet or the adapter is unavailable)

## Motivation

Users upgrading from 2026.5.26 to 2026.6.1 who previously used Google/Gemini as their memorySearch provider see `Dirty: yes` permanently with `reason: "index was built for model gemini-embedding-001, expected "` — the empty `expected` field makes the index unrecoverable. PR #90042 fixed the root cause at `createWithAdapter` (provider initialization), but the identity check can still produce empty-string `expectedModel` before provider init completes, or when the adapter registry hasn't loaded yet.

## Changes

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.ts`

1. Replace `params.provider ? params.provider.model : "fts-only"` with `params.provider?.model?.trim() || "fts-only"`

**File**: `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts`

1. Add 3 tests covering empty-string, whitespace-only, and non-fts mismatch scenarios

No new imports, no config changes, no default surface changes.

## Verification

- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts` — 12 passed (9 original + 3 new)
- `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/embeddings.test.ts` — 10 passed (including PR #90042's 2 backfill tests)

## Real Behavior Proof

**Behavior addressed**: `expectedModel` in `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState` can become an empty string `""` when `provider.model` is empty, causing permanent identity mismatch (`"index was built for model X, expected "`) and blocking vector search recovery.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (Ubuntu), Node.js 22.19+, local checkout on branch `fix/memory-identity-empty-model-90787`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
```
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts --run
http_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 https_proxy=http://proxyhk.zte.com.cn:80 pnpm openclaw memory status --deep
```

**Evidence after fix**: The empty-string `expectedModel` bug is an internal identity-check state that manifests before provider initialization (when the adapter registry has not loaded yet). It cannot be directly triggered via `pnpm openclaw memory status --deep` because that command initializes the provider first, populating `provider.model` via adapter.defaultModel. The fix's direct evidence comes from two sources:

1. **Unit test** (terminal output from `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`): directly tests `resolveMemoryIndexIdentityState` with `provider.model = ""` → `expectedModel = "fts-only"` (not empty string):

```
 ✓ falls back to fts-only when provider.model is an empty string
 ✓ reports mismatch when empty-string expected model is compared to a non-fts index
 ✓ falls back to fts-only when provider.model is whitespace-only
```

2. **CLI output** (`pnpm openclaw memory status --deep`): shows the system working correctly after the fix — `provider.model` is populated (not empty), and identity state is readable:

```
Provider: openai (requested: openai)
Model: text-embedding-3-small
Index identity: index metadata is missing
```

**Before-fix comparison** (from issue #90787 reporter, Rocky Linux, v2026.6.1):

| Field | Before (issue #90787) | After (this patch) |
|-------|----------------------|---------------------|
| `expectedModel` | `""` (empty string) | `"fts-only"` (safe fallback) |
| Identity reason | `"index was built for model gemini-embedding-001, expected "` (trailing empty string) | `"index was built for model gemini-embedding-001, expected fts-only"` (readable) |
| Recovery path | None (permanent Dirty, no rebuild clears it) | Reindex with correct provider → Dirty: no |

**Observed result after fix**: Empty-string `provider.model` no longer causes unreadable identity mismatch. The `|| "fts-only"` fallback produces a clean, recoverable state: existing fts-only indexes match cleanly, and non-fts indexes report a readable mismatch reason ("expected fts-only") that points the user toward a reindex instead of a broken permanent-stuck state.

**What was not tested**: The provider auto→openai migration decision (separate product issue with `needs-product-decision` label). This PR only fixes the identity-check layer; the migration logic that unconditionally rewrites `provider: "auto"` to `"openai"` is not changed and requires maintainer product decision per #90787's `clawsweeper:needs-product-decision` label.

**Verification test output** (12 tests, including 3 new):

```
 RUN  v4.1.7

 ✓ |extension-memory| extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-reindex-state.test.ts (12 tests) 40ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)

Key new test assertions:
- "falls back to fts-only when provider.model is an empty string":
    provider.model = "" → expectedModel = "fts-only" → matches fts-only index → status: "valid"
- "reports mismatch when empty-string expected model is compared to a non-fts index":
    provider.model = "" → expectedModel = "fts-only" → mismatched with "text-embedding-3-small" → reason contains "expected fts-only"
- "falls back to fts-only when provider.model is whitespace-only":
    provider.model = "  " → trimmed to "" → expectedModel = "fts-only" → matches fts-only index → status: "valid"
```

## Risks

- **Low risk**: Single-point guard at the identity comparison consumer, preserving existing behavior for all non-empty `provider.model` values.
- **Additive**: The `|| "fts-only"` fallback only activates when `provider.model` is empty or whitespace — previously these produced broken empty-string mismatches. No change for correctly-populated `provider.model`.
- **Complementary with #90042**: PR #90042 fixes `provider.model` at the creation source (`createWithAdapter`); this PR protects the identity check at the consumption layer. Either fix alone reduces the bug surface; together they eliminate it.
- **No config/default surface changes**: No new config keys, env vars, or defaults introduced.
target.author: xydt-tanshanshan

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
